title: Poa minima
intro: Poa minima, also known as the little bluegrass or wheat grass, is a small, perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Poa minima include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Poa minima is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Poa minima is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Poa, which includes over 600 species of grasses. The Poaceae family is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants, commonly referred to as grasses. Poa minima is native to Australia and is often found in dry, rocky areas. The species is considered to be a small, annual grass with a delicate appearance.
